revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = F31-s-N29 t*t:N25 |NAME-EXPLANATION = Mesqet
''Msqt'' }} '''Mesqet''' (in other {{ill|Publication|en|Publication|qid=Q732577|lt=Publication|1=Publication}}s, in some cases also '''Mesket''') designates in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|qid=Q205740|lt=Egyptian mythology|1=Egyptian mythology}} and {{ill|Egyptian astronomy|en|Egyptian astronomy|qid=Q751843|lt=Ancient Egyptian astronomy|1=Egyptian astronomy}} the celestial region just above the {{ill|Horizon|en|Horizon|qid=Q43261|lt=horizon|1=Horizon}} and specifically refers, in contrast to {{ill|Qenqenet|de|Qenqenet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Qenqenet|qid=Q1300614|1=Qenqenet}}, to the initial areas of the {{ill|Duat|en|Duat|qid=Q871389|lt=Duat|1=Duat}}. == Background == [[File:Mock_mirage_of_the_setting_sun.jpg|alt=Mesqet Zone: Sunrise just above the horizon|thumb]] The Mesqet is the place where {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|qid=Q1252904|lt=Ra|1=Ra}}, {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|qid=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}} and the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Dekan-Stern|lt=Decan stars|lt_ja=Dekan-Sterne|pt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qid=Q804527}} are situated shortly after their birth, before they fully appear above the horizon. Mesqet is, in mythological terms, "the place after birth, before the final magnitude is reached" ({{ill|mythology|de|Mythologie|lt=mythological|lt_ja=mythologisch|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mythology|qid=Q9134|1=Mythology}}). The location of Mesqet was long disputed among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptologists|qid=Q145903|1=Egyptology}}, since this area is attested in many texts as "a region in the sky and in the Duat." Mesqet already plays a significant role especially in the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|en|Pyramid Texts|qid=Q645882|lt=Pyramid Texts|1=Pyramid Texts}}. For example, it was believed by {{ill|Kurt Sethe|en|Kurt Sethe|qid=Q70079|lt=Kurt Sethe|1=Kurt Sethe}},Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums.'' Volume 1: ''Text. – Hälfte 1. Spruch 1 – 468 (Pyr. 1 – 905).'' 3rd reprint of the Hinrichs edition, Leipzig 1908; Olms, Hildesheim 2001, ISBN 3-487-02593-0, p. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arielle Kozloff|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun – Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, pp. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronald Wells|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}},Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In {{ill|Studien zur Altägyptischen Kultur|en|Studien zur Altägyptischen Kultur|qid=Q2358456|lt=Studies in Ancient Egyptian Culture|1=Studien zur Altägyptischen Kultur}}, No. 19, 1992, pp. 305–321. that it might be the {{ill|Milky Way|en|Milky Way|qid=Q321|lt=Milky Way|1=Milky Way}}. Subsequent investigations of other texts, however, could no longer maintain this suggestion, as the other evidence clearly referred to a "transit region of the {{ill|sky (planetary)|de|Himmel (planetär)|lt=sky|lt_ja=Himmels|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sky (planetary)|qid=Q527|1=Sky}} and the {{ill|Star|en|Star|qid=Q523|lt=stars|1=Star}}." After extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harco Willems|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, pp. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qid=Q91239|1=Rolf Krauss}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Beliefs in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, pp. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arno Egberts|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}},Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Oosten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, pp. 292–293. the area was narrowed down to the locations of the {{ill|Sunrise|en|Sunrise|qid=Q193294|lt=sunrise|1=Sunrise}}. With the results of the investigations of the {{ill|Papyrus|en|Papyrus|qid=Q125576|lt=Papyrus|1=Papyrus}} {{ill|pBM 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pBM 47.218.50|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} and the related subject of the Mesqet sanctuaries ({{ill|Mesqet sanctuaries|de|Mesqet-Heiligtümer|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesqet sanctuaries|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}),Jean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an: Brooklyn Museum papyrus 47.218.50'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'étude.'' Volume 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale, Cairo 1972, pp. 93–94. it quickly became clear that these newer assumptions correspond with the mention in the Brooklyn Papyrus, which explicitly names only the cardinal directions east and west. Finally, the revision of the {{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|qid=Q2005559|lt=Book of Nut|1=Book of Nut}} confirmed the precise allocation to the regions of the exit gates of the Duat, which are located just above the horizon. == See also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian gods|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian gods|qid=Q1076616|1=List of Egyptian deities}} == Literature == * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qid=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 136–137 == References == {{translated page|de|Mesqet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{wikidata link|Q1922516|de|Mesqet|pt|Mesqet}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
